title: Athroandra macrophylla
intro: Athroandra macrophylla, also known as the "Kapok tree," is a species of plant that belongs to the family Staphylocaryaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian cultures for its medicinal properties, exhibiting health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, which can aid in the treatment of fever and pain. The bioactive constituents of Athroandra macrophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Athroandra macrophylla is a species of plant in the family Icacinaceae. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the order Icacinales. The family Icacinaceae is a group of plants that are native to various parts of the world, including Africa and Asia. The Icacinaceae family is thought to have originated over 100 million years ago.
